channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Attacks by Sudanese RSF paramilitaries leave hundreds dead in White Nile State
item.description: Sudanese officials and rights groups say insurgent attacks by the paramilitary Rapid Support Force have killed hundreds of civilians, including infants, in White Nile state.
item.dc:creator: Fatma Khaled, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hamas says it will free 6 living hostages and hand over four bodies, accelerating Gaza releases
item.description: A top Hamas leader says the militant group will release six living Israeli hostages on Saturday and the bodies of four others on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Toronto upside-down plane crash: Delta passenger records himself climbing out of aircraft in dramatic video
item.description: All 80 people aboard the Delta plane that flipped upside down and crash landed in Toronto survived, but three, including a child, are critically injured.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top Russian, US officials meet in Saudi Arabia to begin talks on Ukraine war without officials from Kyiv
item.description: Senior Russian and American officials met in Saudi Arabia on Tuesday to begin talks on improving relations and negotiating an end to Moscow's war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese navy helicopter flies within 10 feet of Philippine patrol plane over disputed shoal
item.description: A Chinese navy helicopter has flown within 10 feet of a Philippine patrol plane in a disputed area of the the South China Sea, highlighting Beijing's use of force in asserting its claims to the strategic waterway.
item.dc:creator: Joeal Calupitan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Turkey detains 282 suspects in a dayslong operation against Kurdish insurgents
item.description: Turkish police have detained 282 suspects in a large-scale operation targeting the banned Kurdistan Workers’ Party, or PKK.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: European leaders want a say in talks to end Russia's war in Ukraine. The US is brushing them off
item.description: PARIS (AP) — European leaders insisted Monday they must have a say in international talks to end the war in Ukraine despite the clear message from both Washington and Moscow that there was no role for them as yet in negotiations that could shape the future of the continent.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and RAF CASERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shaheen, Tillis and Bennet tour Ukraine amid Trump peace push
item.description: Sens. Jeanne Shaheen (D-N.H.), Thom Tillis (R-N.C.) and Michael Bennet (D-Colo.) all recently took a trip to Ukraine according to a page on the Senate Foreign Relations Committee's website. The senators finished the visit to Ukraine’s capital city, Kyiv, on Monday, according to the page.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Tourist loses both hands while snorkeling in Turks and Caicos after shark attacks during failed photo op
item.description: A woman from Canada lost both her hands when she was attacked by a shark after trying to take a picture with the creature while swimming in Turks and Caicos.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump speaks with Macron ahead of European leaders meeting on Ukraine
item.description: President Trump spoke with French President Emmanuel Macron ahead of a European leaders' meeting about the war in Ukraine, as the Trump administration is pressing forward with plans to negotiate an end to the war.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Delta jet flips upside down on a snowy Toronto runway and all 80 aboard survive
item.description: A Delta Air Lines plane has flipped upon arrival at Toronto’s Pearson Airport and ended up on its roof, injuring 18 people.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Delta jet crashes at Canada's Toronto Pearson Airport; crews responding to plane flipped upside down
item.description: Officials confirmed to Fox News Digital that Delta plane crashed at Toronto Pearson Airport on Monday afternoon. The CRJ-900 jet, operating as Delta 4819, departed from Minneapolis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Argentina's Milei faces fraud probe after boosting a crypto coin that crashed hours after its launch
item.description: A judge in Argentina has been selected to investigate allegations of fraud against President Javier Milei for his brief promotion of a cryptocurrency that collapsed in value hours after it was launched last week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukraine presses Europe to nominate representative for Russia talks
item.description: Ukraine is urging Europe to quickly select a representative for potential talks with the U.S. and Russia on ending Kyiv's war with Russia. “It should be a quickly made decision,” Igor Zhovkva, the deputy head of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky’s office, told Bloomberg.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: Many weary Ukrainians long for an end to the war but now fear it will come on unfavorable terms
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — After nearly three years of living under constant threat of Russian airstrikes while their troops fight a grinding campaign against Russia's invasion, many Ukrainians long for an end to the war — but now fear it could come on unfavorable terms. Top U.S.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy travels to UAE as momentum grows for talks to end Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: ABU DHABI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met with the leader of the United Arab Emirates on Monday as momentum grows for potential peace talks ending Russia's war on the country. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Retired general: Putin will likely 'wait out' Trump term and take Ukraine
item.description: Retired U.S. Army Gen. Jack Keane said he suspects Russian President Vladimir Putin will wait for President Trump to leave office before trying to topple the government in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Zelenskyy not yet signing US economic agreement 'short-sighted,' White House official says
item.description: A White House national security official said Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy "is being short-sighted" in not signing a rare earth minerals deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US leaders in Saudi Arabia for peace talks on Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he will not accept a deal without a seat at the negotiating table.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Here are some takeaways from the first month of Trump's Mideast diplomacy
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — When Hamas threatened to call off the planned release of three Israeli hostages last week, U.S. President Donald Trump stepped into the picture with an unexpected ultimatum.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rwanda-backed M23 rebels occupy a 2nd major city in Congo's mineral-rich east
item.description: BUKAVU, Congo (AP) — Rwanda-backed rebels have occupied a second major city in mineral-rich eastern Congo, the government said Sunday, as M23 rebels confirmed they were in the city to restore order after it was abandoned by Congolese forces.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU, PASCAL CINAMULA and JANVIER BARHAHIGA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Earth’s solid metal core may be soft on the outside: Study
item.description: Rather than being perfectly ball-shaped, the Earth's inner core may actually resemble peaks and valleys.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Woman killed as Israeli forces fire on returnees to southern Lebanon ahead of ceasefire deadline
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — A woman was killed and several other people wounded Sunday when Israeli forces opened fire on a group of residents attempting to return to the village of Houla in southern Lebanon, the Lebanese state-run news agency reported.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu signals he's moving ahead with Trump's idea to transfer Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Sunday signaled that he was moving ahead with U.S. President Donald Trump's proposal to transfer the Palestinian population out of Gaza, calling it “the only viable plan to enable a different future" for the region.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Ukrainian spiritual leader says Russian Orthodox Church extension of Kremlin
item.description: A senior Ukrainian Orthodox Church official recently warned that Russian President Vladimir Putin thinks the Kremlin is in a 'holy war' with the West.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gold mine collapse kills 42 in Mali
item.description: Late on Saturday, Malian television announced the collapse of the site at Bilali Koto in the commune of Dabia, with a provisional death toll of 42 and many injured.
item.dc:creator: BABA AHMED, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: White South Africans gather in support of Trump and his claims that they are victims of racism
item.description: PRETORIA, South Africa (AP) — Some white South Africans showed support for President Donald Trump on Saturday and gathered at the U.S. Embassy in Pretoria to claim they are victims of racism by their own government.
item.dc:creator: ALFONSO NQUNJANA and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Students in Iran continue protests over 19-year-old’s murder on campus for second day
item.description: Student protesters in Iran called out officials at the University of Tehran for a second day on Saturday following the murder of a business student on campus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Doctors order 'absolute rest' for Pope Francis while he's being treated for a respiratory infection
item.description: ROME (AP) — Doctors on Saturday prescribed “absolute rest” for Pope Francis and modified the treatment for his respiratory tract infection, the Vatican said, a day after the 88-year-old pope was admitted to a hospital following a weeklong bout of bronchitis.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Netanyahu will reportedly hold a security meeting at Trump's deadline
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu is expected to hold a security meeting at 12 p.m. eastern, President Trump's deadline for Hamas to release the remaining hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel releases Palestinian prisoners linked to suicide bombings, attempted murders
item.description: Palestinian prisoners linked to suicide bombings and attempted murders against Israelis are among the 369 released Saturday by Israel in exchange for hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelenskyy to Vance: Ukraine wants 'security guarantees' as Trump seeks to end Ukraine-Russia war
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday that his country wants “security guarantees” before any talks with Russia, as the Trump administration presses both countries to find a quick endgame to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A drone pierced the outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant. Radiation levels are normal
item.description: CHERNOBYL NUCLEAR POWER STATION, Ukraine (AP) — A drone armed with a warhead hit the protective outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, punching a hole in the structure and briefly starting a fire, in an attack Kyiv blamed on Russia. The Kremlin denied it was responsible.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and EFREM LUKATSKY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, Modi meet at Oval Office as US weighs tariffs
item.description: Before speaking with Trump, Modi met separately with Tesla CEO Elon Musk, a Trump administration official.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Steel tariffs could disrupt international bridge expansion
item.description: The steel 2-lane Starr-Camargo International Bridge in Rio Grande City, Texas, is gearing up for an expansion of lanes, however they might not be steel after 25% tariffs were imposed by the Trump administration, the bridge's owner Sam Vale told Border Report on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says he and Putin agreed to begin ‘negotiations’ on ending Ukraine war
item.description: Trump says he and Russian leader Vladimir Putin have agreed to begin "negotiations" on ending the Ukraine war.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, ERIC TUCKER and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine selling arms to cartels? Tucker Carlson claims rebutted
item.description: Carlson claims U.S. intelligence agencies know about the billions in transactions to arm “our actual enemies.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation
